title: ulmus campestris f. concavifolia
intro: Ulmus campestris f. concavifolia, also known as the Concave-leaved Elm, is a variant of the Field Elm (Ulmus campestris) tree, characterized by its concave-shaped leaves. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of Ulmus campestris f. concavifolia have been used in herbal medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Ulmus campestris f. concavifolia can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat various ailments.
